STIPULATION (JOINT) TO AMEND BRIEFING SCHEDULE AND CONTINUE HEARING ON DEFTS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRKE TO JUL-21-11; POS FILED BY CREATIVE LOAFING ATLANTA LAGON, CHANTE WHEATLEY, THOMAS GRIGSBY B, CALVIN GRIGSBY & ASSOCIATES, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHANTE LAGON, THOMAS WHEATLEY, AND CREATIVE LOAFING ATLANTA'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT (CCP SECTION 425.16). NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) IS GRANTED. FIRST THIS CASE IMPLICATES BOTH CCP SECTION 425.16(E)(2) AND (E)(4). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DEFAMATION AND INVASION OF PRIVACY BASED SOLELY ON AN ARTICLE THAT WAS PUBLISHED BY THE SECOND MOST BROADLY DISTRIBUTED NEWSPAPER IN GEORGIA. THE ARTICLE WAS WRITTEN TO REPORT ON THE ATLANTA AIRPORT BOND DEAL WHICH CLEARLY IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC INTEREST. ACCORDINGLY, THE COMPLAINT AND CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. SECOND, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN ANY PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. FIRST, THE ARTICLE IS SUBSTANTIALLY TRUE AND THEREFORE PRIVILEGED UNDER THE CONSTITUTION. RINGER ASSOC. V. MARYLAND CASUALTY CO. (2008) 80 CAL.APP.4TH 1165, 1180. SECOND, THE ARTICLE IS PROTECTED BY STATUTE AS A FAIR AND TRUE REPORT OF OFFICIAL PROCEEDINGS UNDER CCP SECTION 47(D). SUCH REPORTS MUST MERELY REFLECT THE "GIST" OF THE OFFICIAL PROCEEDING AND RECORDS. JENNINGS V. TELEGRAM-TRIBUNE CO. (1985) 164 CAL.APP.3D 119, 125. MOREOVER PLAINTIFFS DO NOT DISPUTE THAT A STATEMENT IS ABSOLUTELY PROTECTED AS A FAIR REPORT UNDER SECTION 47 OR THE FIRST AMENDMENT IF THE STATEMENT CAPTURES THE GIST AND STING OF THE OFFICIAL REPORTS OR PROCEEDINGS, EVEN IF THERE ARE SLIGHT INACCURACIES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DEFENDANTS CHANTE LAGON, THOMAS WHEATLEY, AND CREATIVE LOAFING ATLANTA'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16).

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) IS GRANTED.
FIRST THIS CASE IMPLICATES BOTH CCP SECTION 425.16(e)(2) and (e)(4). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DEFAMATION AND INVASION OF PRIVACY BASED SOLELY ON AN ARTICLE THAT WAS PUBLISHED BY THE SECOND MOST BROADLY DISTRIBUTED NEWSPAPER IN GEORGIA. THE ARTICLE WAS WRITTEN TO REPORT ON THE ATLANTA AIRPORT BOND DEAL WHICH CLEARLY IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC INTEREST. ACCORDINGLY, THE COMPLAINT AND CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY.
SECOND, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN ANY PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. FIRST, THE ARTICLE IS SUBSTANTIALLY TRUE AND THEREFORE PRIVILEGED UNDER THE CONSTITUTION. RINGER ASSOC. V. MARYLAND CASUALTY CO. (2008) 80 CAL.APP.4TH 1165, 1180. SECOND, THE ARTICLE IS PROTECTED BY STATUTE AS A FAIR AND TRUE REPORT OF OFFICIAL PROCEEDINGS UNDER CCP SECTION 47(d). SUCH REPORTS MUST MERELY REFLECT THE "GIST" OF THE OFFICIAL PROCEEDING AND RECORDS. JENNINGS V. TELEGRAM-TRIBUNE CO. (1985) 164 CAL.APP.3D 119, 125. MOREOVER PLAINTIFFS DO NOT DISPUTE THAT A STATEMENT IS ABSOLUTELY PROTECTED AS A FAIR REPORT UNDER SECTION 47 OR THE FIRST AMENDMENT IF THE STATEMENT CAPTURES THE GIST AND STING OF THE OFFICIAL REPORTS OR PROCEEDINGS, EVEN IF THERE ARE SLIGHT INACCURACIES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
